prompt: Publish this HTML and give me the URL
example:
const { data, error } = await dropthis.publish("

Hello

"); if (!error) console.log(data.url);

prompt: Upload my dist folder to dropthis
Update drop_abc123 with new content from ./build
example: const { data, error } = await dropthis.deploy("drop_abc123", "./build", { ifRevision: 3 });

prompt: How do I set up authentication for the dropthis SDK?
expected_tool: new Dropthis({ apiKey }) or DROPTHIS_API_KEY env var

prompt: Publish this page with a password so only people with the password can see it
Get details about a specific deployment dep_xyz789 on drop_abc123
example:
const { data, error } = await dropthis.deployments.get("drop_abc123", "dep_xyz789");
if (!error) console.log(data.status, data.sizeBytes);

prompt: How do I handle errors when publishing?
Publish from a CI pipeline using an environment variable for the key
expected_tool: new Dropthis() with DROPTHIS_API_KEY env var
expected_input: No explicit apiKey -- reads from env

prompt: Change the title of drop_abc123 without uploading new content
Show me the deployment history for drop_abc123
Create a new API key labeled 'production'
example:
const { data, error } = await dropthis.apiKeys.create({ label: "production" });
if (!error) console.log("Key:", data.key);

prompt: Publish a small app made of index.html, style.css, and app.js together as one drop
expected_input: { kind: "files", files: [...] } or string[] of paths
expectations: Uses dropthis.publish() with { kind: "files", files: [...] } OR a string[] of file paths
Each file is its own entry in the files array (path + content)
Does NOT inline style.css or app.js into the index.html string
Sets entry: "index.html" (or relies on the index.html default)

prompt: Publish a copy of the page at https://example.com/report.html and give me the URL
expected_input: a bare http(s) URL string (or { kind: "source_url", sourceUrl })
expectations: Passes the URL string directly to dropthis.publish() (the server fetches it via source_url)
Does NOT fetch/download the URL itself before publishing
Does NOT pass the URL as inline { kind: "content" }

prompt: Update a drop safely with optimistic concurrency
